samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for pulse detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </input>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the pulse detection process.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State indicating the end of a pulse.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="data_out_reg">Store the output signal.</sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module pulse_detect detects pulses in the input signal data_in. A pulse is defined as a sequence of 1s and 0s, where the sequence starts with a 1, followed by 0s, and ends with a 1. The output signal data_out is 1 when the pulse detection process is in state S3, which indicates the end of a pulse. The module uses a finite state machine (FSM) to detect pulses. The states of the FSM are S0, S1, S2, and S3. The FSM transitions between states based on the value of the input signal data_in. The module resets to state S0 when the reset signal is active. The output signal data_out is 0 in states S0, S1, and S2, and 1 in state S3.
    </implementation>
</module>
